name: NARPS — Mixed Gambles (Neuroimaging Analysis Replication and Prediction Study)
description: fMRI from 108 participants performing a mixed-gambles task: on each trial they accept or reject a 50/50 gamble with a potential monetary gain and an equal-or-smaller loss — the classic paradigm for studying loss aversion and value-based decision making. Fully BIDS-formatted with fMRIPrep derivatives, NARPS is an ideal teaching dataset for model-based fMRI: fit a participant's loss-aversion parameter from their choices, then map trial-wise subjective value onto the brain.
citation: Botvinik-Nezer R, Iwanir R, Holzmeister F, et al. (2019). fMRI data of mixed gambles from the Neuroimaging Analysis Replication and Prediction Study. Scientific Data, 6, 106.

publications.title: Variability in the analysis of a single neuroimaging dataset by many teams
